response.justification: Strong senior IC with 15+ years building software across web, mobile, embedded, and GIS, plus solid Linux/open-source credentials (DLang, Node, self-hosted GitLab, GISCollective founder). However, the role is executive-level Head of Development requiring formal team leadership, architectural ownership of client engagements, and German at C1 — and the resume shows only basic German, no explicit people-management or tech-lead titles, and limited Python. Worth a screening call to probe leadership scope and German trajectory before ruling out.
strengths: Deep open-source/Linux background and founder of GISCollective, aligning with digital-sovereignty focus
15+ years across diverse stacks with architecture-level work on high-throughput and event-driven systems
Demonstrated mentoring/teaching via workshops, Selenium course, Refactoring Dojos, and conference talks
concerns: German listed as only Basic, well below the required C1 threshold
No formal Head of / Tech Lead / line-management title — leadership of an interdisciplinary team is unproven
Python and UML are not visible in the skills or project history despite being listed as desired

key_factors.factor: Lack of formal leadership/management track record
